FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Regional consolidation accelerates with First Hawaiian's $2B TriCo deal, while macro headwinds (rising rate-hike odds) clash with near-term Q2 earnings strength from dealmaking and geopolitical volatility.

bullets.text: First Hawaiian Bank acquiring TriCo Bancshares for $2B, closing by year-end—marks mainland U.S. re-entry after BNP Paribas exit and signals renewed regional M&A appetite.
Fed rate-hike odds for July rising on oil spike from Strait of Hormuz tension; direct headwind to bank NIM compression despite near-term earnings tailwinds.
Q2 bank revenues expected to pop from SpaceX IPO advisory fees, geopolitical trading, and commercial lending pickup—but offset by longer-term rate pressure.
CFPB Trump pick Brian Johnson wins 60-day post-confirmation review on staff cuts; gives incoming administration discretion over bureau workforce reduction.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Russell Investments' $580B CalPERS acquisition and RIA consolidation surge dominate; litigation risk emerges at Betterment.

bullets.text: CalPERS acquiring Russell Investments in landmark deal—Russell sheds debt, adopts non-profit structure mimicking Vanguard's playbook for reputational lift.
RIA consolidation accelerating: Hightower posts organic growth highs, Choreo closes fourth deal post-SpaceX, ex-Fido CTO raises $7.7M for new platform, Wealthfront crosses $100B AUM.
Carson Group restructures into separate W-2 and independent channels to sharpen focus; Summitry (Aspen-backed, $4B AUM) names two co-CEOs after founder steps to chairman role.
Betterment hit with class action over cash sweep accounts—alleged undisclosed profits from sweeping client cash into non-interest-bearing deposits without compensation.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: U.S. P&C industry hits decade-high profitability amid pricing discipline, but geopolitical risk in Hormuz Strait threatens marine underwriters while new players target California homeowners gap.

bullets.text: U.S. P&C posted strongest underwriting profit in 10 years with $84B gain over two years and lowest combined ratio on $1T in direct premiums—but performance diverged sharply across lines.
UAE tanker attacks in Hormuz Strait (21% of global crude corridor) killed one seafarer and will likely spike war-risk insurance premiums and marine underwriter P&L volatility.
Saudi Re picks up 22.5% stake in Lloyd's operator AdA Risk for £8.95M, signaling regional appetite to deepen London market exposure with regulatory blessing already in hand.
RockRose Risk launched AI-driven homeowners product in California tied to wildfire mitigation incentives, filling void left by traditional carriers retreating from the state.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Four stories spanning private debt appetite, CMBS distress signals, fintech expansion, and regulatory headwinds across specialty finance.

bullets.text: North America remains LP favorite for private debt despite market volatility; AlbaCore notes clear divergence between performing and distressed credit performance.
CMBS special servicing rate climbed in June 2026 as retail, office, and lodging loans deteriorate—signaling potential workout activity ahead in property-backed securities.
Klarna hit with Dutch class action lawsuit, adding regulatory pressure on BNPL unicorn in core European market just as fintech litigation risk intensifies.

PE & Deals.lede: PE deployment accelerating across GP financing innovation, secondaries expansion, and energy infrastructure—Arctos debuts $6.2B self-liquidating structure while mega-funds target Williams.

bullets.text: Arctos Partners closed a $6.2B GP financing deal using a novel 'self-liquidating' Keystone structure—first deployment of this model in GP-financing markets, allowing capital returns to LPs while maintaining portfolio exposure.
Moelis hired a four-person team from Evercore to launch LP-led secondaries practice, betting on expansion in this high-growth segment of the secondaries market.
Blackstone, Apollo, and KKR co-invested $5.34B for minority stakes in Williams' five Power Innovation projects, signaling appetite among mega-funds for large-cap energy infrastructure.

Markets & Macro.lede: Bank earnings season opens with high stakes; structural tailwinds (NIMs, AI efficiency) clash with QT risks and portfolio rotation dynamics.

bullets.text: Bank earnings kick off Tuesday with market priced for perfection—strong NIMs and cost cuts driving record profits, but disappointment risk is real.
QE rewired bank funding models to depend on central bank liquidity; QT poses underestimated risks as policy makers unwind without accounting for structural balance sheet changes.
Small caps rallying hard (Russell 2000 best year since 2003) as money rotates from mega-cap tech into AI capex beneficiaries and better valuations.
ECB tapping 36 banks (including skeptics) to pilot digital euro in 2025—testing both tech and regulatory viability in real-world conditions.

Newsletters.lede: Big bank earnings dominate today's reads, with secondary themes around ETF redemptions, RIA workflows, and structural shifts in education finance.

bullets.text: Five of six largest US banks report Q2 earnings tomorrow—watch for credit quality, deposit dynamics, and consumer health signals across lending, trading, and wealth management.
ETF flows swung negative in rare week; redemptions hit equity, bond, and alternative products as market volatility or rotation triggered the shift.
Vanguard and Envestnet launched joint tools to reduce tax friction on model portfolio conversions—addresses key RIA workflow pain point.
Student loan policy changes are forcing advisors to rebuild college planning playbooks, reshaping education financing product demand.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: 4 stories spanning incentive misalignment, computational economics, market structure, and AI strategy—from NBA shooting to Apple's OpenAI lawsuit.

bullets.text: NBA players gaming shooting percentages by taking low-value end-of-quarter heaves: classic principal-agent problem where individual metrics misalign with team performance.
Cowen's thesis: competitive markets exist only if P ≠ NP; if P = NP, firms can efficiently detect and sustain cartels, collapsing equilibrium competition.
Groq's Jonathan Ross secured $20B NVIDIA deal via unconventional financing (Grok Bonds) after near-bankruptcy—startup capital creativity in AI infrastructure.
Apple's OpenAI lawsuit over employee trade-secret theft masks a bigger problem: no coherent AI strategy while rivals race ahead.

all.lede: Regional bank M&A kicks off with First Hawaiian's $2B TriCo deal, while major bank earnings dominate the week ahead amid questions about credit quality and the Fed's QT risks.

bullets.text: First Hawaiian Bank acquiring TriCo Bancshares for $2B—marks First Hawaiian's return to mainland U.S. market after BNP Paribas exit, closing by year-end.
Five of six largest U.S. banks report Q2 earnings tomorrow; results will signal credit quality, deposit trends, and consumer health across lending, trading, and wealth management.
QE structurally rewired bank balance sheets and funding models, making quantitative tightening riskier than policymakers realize because banks now depend on central bank liquidity support.
